falsification_conditions.condition: S&P 500 drops by 5% from current level within 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A sharp decline would contradict the bottom pattern and blowoff score, indicating continued bearish momentum rather than a reversal.

falsification_conditions.condition: Volume on the S&P 500 surges to 200% above the 20-day average within 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: Excessive volume would signal a blowoff top (not a bottom), invalidating the pattern match to a prior bottom and suggesting panic selling.

falsification_conditions.condition: MACD crosses below the signal line and falls below -0.5 within 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A bearish MACD divergence would confirm ongoing downward momentum, directly contradicting the pattern's implication of a potential reversal.

doctrine: If you can't list what would prove your call wrong, you don't have a real call.
